question: [File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / take] Can an unprivileged maker acting as a buy-offer maker under the precondition that the same seller address is already the subject of an outer take (wasLocked=true from a prior nested tExchange call) trigger a reentrant inner take via IBuyCallback.onBuy that sets tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true) returning wasLocked2=true, causing the inner take to skip the lock-clear branch (if (!wasLocked2)), so that after the inner take completes the outer take's wasLocked=false branch clears the lock prematurely before the outer sell callback fires, violating the invariant that the seller must remain locked for the entire duration of all callbacks in the outer take, causing scoped impact: seller can be liquidated between the outer buy callback and the outer sell callback? Proof idea: fuzz nested take depth and assert liquidationLocked(id, seller) is true at every point between tExchange set and the final health check in the outer take.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / repay] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that their position is unhealthy after partial debt reduction trigger the sequence repay(units, onBehalf, callback, data) which calls UtilsLib.toUint128 to reduce position[id][onBehalf].debt before any liquidation lock is set, then fires IRepayCallback.onRepay during which a liquidator calls liquidate() on the same borrower (no tExchange lock exists in repay), violating the invariant that a borrower's debt and collateral state must be atomically consistent before any external call, causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral against a debt that has already been partially reduced by the repay, resulting in over-seizure relative to the actual remaining debt? Proof idea: unit test repay with a callback that reenters liquidate and assert seized collateral value does not exceed remaining debt after repay.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that a borrower has multiple collateral types trigger liquidate() which iterates the collateralBitmap using UtilsLib.msb and UtilsLib.clearBit to compute maxDebt and badDebt, then transfers seized collateral via safeTransfer before calling ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate, during which the callback reenters liquidate() on the same borrower with a different collateralIndex, violating the invariant that bad debt must be realized exactly once per liquidation event and that _position.debt must not be double-decremented, causing scoped impact: double bad-debt realization reduces lender credit twice for the same underlying debt? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test asserting sum of all credit reductions equals exactly the bad debt realized, not a multiple.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: zeroFloorSub / take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the buyer has existing debt trigger take() which computes buyerCreditIncrease = UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub(units, buyerPos.debt) from the stale pre-callback debt value, then fires IBuyCallback.onBuy during which the buyer's debt is further reduced via a reentrant repay(), so that the outer take's already-committed buyerPos.debt -= (units - buyerCreditIncrease) underflows or double-counts the debt reduction, violating the invariant that every credit increase must correspond to exactly one valid debt reduction or new credit creation, causing scoped impact: buyer receives credit units without a corresponding debt obligation or asset payment? Proof idea: fuzz buyer debt and repay amount inside callback and assert position[id][buyer].credit + position[id][buyer].debt equals pre-take credit + pre-take debt + net units.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / take (sell-side callback)] Can an unprivileged maker of a sell offer under the precondition that the seller has a callback registered trigger take() which sets tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true), completes the buy callback and token transfers, then fires ISellCallback.onSell during which the sell callback reenters take() with the same seller as the new sell-offer maker, causing tExchange to return wasLocked2=true so the inner take does NOT clear the lock, and the inner take's health check uses liquidationLocked(id, seller)=true to skip the health check, violating the invariant that every debt-increasing take must pass a health check before completion, causing scoped impact: seller accumulates unbounded debt without a health check because the lock suppresses the isHealthy call? Proof idea: assert that after all nested takes complete, isHealthy(market, id, seller) is true.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: mulDivDown / mulDivUp / take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that buyerPrice is near WAD and units is near type(uint128).max trigger take() where buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD) and sellerAssets = units.mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) are computed before callbacks, then during IBuyCallback.onBuy the callback reenters take() with a different offer to change claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] state, so that the outer take's claimableSettlementFee[loanToken] += buyerAssets - sellerAssets accumulates fees based on stale asset values, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must equal the sum of all (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) across all takes, causing scoped impact: fee accounting desync allows overclaim or underclaim of settlement fees? Proof idea: fuzz units and prices across nested takes and assert claimableSettlementFee equals sum of all individual fee deltas.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: msb / clearBit / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that a borrower has exactly MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER (16) activated collaterals trigger liquidate() which iterates _collateralBitmap using UtilsLib.msb and UtilsLib.clearBit on a local copy of the bitmap, then calls ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate during which the callback calls supplyCollateral to add a new collateral (setting a new bit via UtilsLib.setBit on the live _position.collateralBitmap), so that the outer liquidate's subsequent _position.collateralBitmap.clearBit(collateralIndex) operates on the now-modified live bitmap, violating the invariant that collateralBitmap must accurately reflect all and only activated collateral positions, causing scoped impact: bitmap desync leaves a collateral bit set with zero balance or clears a bit for a non-zero collateral? Proof idea: assert after liquidate that for all i, (collateralBitmap bit i set) iff (position.collateral[i] > 0).

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: toUint128 / claimContinuousFee] Can an unprivileged fee claimer under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit is near type(uint128).max trigger claimContinuousFee() which calls UtilsLib.toUint128(amount) to subtract from _marketState.continuousFeeCredit, _marketState.totalUnits, and _marketState.withdrawable, then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer which triggers an ERC777 tokensReceived hook on the receiver, during which the hook reenters claimContinuousFee() again before the first call's state is finalized, violating the invariant that continuousFeeCredit must never go below zero and totalUnits must remain consistent with outstanding credit, causing scoped impact: double-claim of continuous fees drains loan token balance beyond what is owed to fee claimer? Proof idea: assert contract loanToken balance decreases by exactly amount per claim call and continuousFeeCredit is non-negative after each call.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: countBits / setBit / supplyCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they have MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER - 1 activated collaterals and the collateral token is ERC777-compatible trigger supplyCollateral() which calls UtilsLib.setBit to set a new bit and UtilsLib.countBits(newCollateralBitmap) to check the limit, then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom which triggers an ERC777 tokensToSend hook on the sender, during which the hook reenters supplyCollateral() for a different collateral index before the first call's bitmap update is committed, violating the invariant that a borrower must never have more than MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER activated collaterals, causing scoped impact: borrower activates more than 16 collaterals, causing msb iteration in isHealthy and liquidate to access out-of-bounds collateralParams indices? Proof idea: assert countBits(position[id][borrower].collateralBitmap) <= MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER after any sequence of supplyCollateral calls.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / take (buy callback reenters liquidate on buyer)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the buyer has existing debt and is unhealthy trigger take() which sets tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true) locking only the seller, then fires IBuyCallback.onBuy during which a liquidator reenters liquidate() on the buyer (who is NOT locked by tExchange), violating the invariant that a buyer's position must not be liquidated while their debt is being modified by an in-progress take, causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes buyer's collateral while the outer take is simultaneously reducing buyer's debt, leaving buyer's position in an inconsistent state with debt double-decremented? Proof idea: assert position[id][buyer].debt is non-negative and consistent with collateral after concurrent take and liquidate on buyer.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: zeroFloorSub / take (seller credit decrease)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the seller has existing credit trigger take() which computes sellerCreditDecrease = UtilsLib.min(units, sellerPos.credit) and sellerDebtIncrease = units - sellerCreditDecrease from stale pre-callback values, then fires IBuyCallback.onBuy during which the seller's credit is increased via a reentrant take (seller buys units), so that the outer take's sellerPos.credit -= sellerCreditDecrease underflows because sellerPos.credit was already modified, violating the invariant that credit must never underflow and total credit accounting must remain consistent, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow reverts the outer take or corrupts sellerPos.credit to a near-max uint128 value? Proof idea: fuzz seller credit changes inside buy callback and assert sellerPos.credit is non-negative after outer take completes.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: mulDivDown / liquidate (lossFactor update)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that _marketState.totalUnits equals badDebt (all units are bad debt) trigger liquidate() which computes _marketState.lossFactor = UtilsLib.toUint128(type(uint128).max - (type(uint128).max - _lossFactor).mulDivDown(_totalUnits - badDebt, _totalUnits)) where _totalUnits - badDebt = 0, causing a division by zero or setting lossFactor to type(uint128).max, then fires ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate during which the callback reenters take() which checks lossFactor < type(uint128).max and reverts, violating the invariant that the market must remain operable after bad debt realization unless lossFactor is genuinely maxed out, causing scoped impact: market permanently frozen for all takers after a single full-bad-debt liquidation? Proof idea: assert take does not revert after liquidate when totalUnits > badDebt.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / multicall] Can an unprivileged user under the precondition that they control both a buy offer and a sell offer for the same market trigger multicall([take(buyOffer), take(sellOffer)]) where the first take sets tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller1, true) and the second take sets tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller2, true), and if seller1 == seller2 the second take returns wasLocked=true so it will not clear the lock, but the first take's wasLocked=false causes it to clear the lock after the second take completes, violating the invariant that the liquidation lock must persist for the full duration of each take's callback window, causing scoped impact: seller can be liquidated between the two takes in the multicall sequence? Proof idea: assert liquidationLocked(id, seller) is true at every point during both takes in a multicall.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: zeroFloorSub / liquidate (badDebt computation)] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that a borrower has multiple collaterals with varying maxLif values trigger liquidate() which computes badDebt using UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub(badDebt, collateral.mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, maxLif)) iteratively, then fires ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate during which the callback calls supplyCollateral to add more collateral to the borrower, so that the outer liquidate's already-computed badDebt value is stale and does not reflect the new collateral, violating the invariant that bad debt must equal the portion of debt not covered by any collateral at maxLif, causing scoped impact: bad debt is over-realized, reducing lender credit more than the actual uncovered debt? Proof idea: assert badDebt realized equals max(0, debt - sum(collateral_i * price_i / maxLif_i)) after all callbacks complete.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: clearBit / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they have exactly 1 unit of collateral at a given index trigger withdrawCollateral() which computes newCollateral = _position.collateral[collateralIndex] - assets = 0 and calls UtilsLib.clearBit to clear the bitmap bit, then calls isHealthy() which iterates the updated bitmap (bit now cleared), then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer which triggers an ERC777 tokensReceived hook on the receiver, during which the hook reenters withdrawCollateral() for the same index (collateral is now 0, so subtraction underflows), violating the invariant that collateral must never go below zero and bitmap must accurately reflect non-zero collateral, causing scoped impact: arithmetic underflow wraps collateral to near-max uint128, giving borrower phantom collateral? Proof idea: assert position[id][borrower].collateral[i] >= 0 and bitmap bit i is clear iff collateral[i] == 0 after any withdrawCollateral sequence.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / take (permanent lock via wasLocked=true)] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that they can initiate a take where the seller is already locked (wasLocked=true from an outer take) trigger a reentrant inner take via IBuyCallback.onBuy where wasLocked2=true, causing the inner take to skip the lock-clear (if (!wasLocked2) is false), and then the outer take's wasLocked=false causes it to clear the lock, but if the outer take reverts after clearing the lock (e.g., due to a failed health check), the transient storage is left in an inconsistent state, violating the invariant that tExchange must leave the lock in a defined state after any revert, causing scoped impact: seller's liquidation lock is permanently cleared or set incorrectly after a revert, allowing or blocking liquidation incorrectly? Proof idea: assert liquidationLocked(id, seller) is false after any reverted take sequence.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: mulDivUp / repay (withdrawable inflation)] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that they have debt trigger repay() which calls UtilsLib.toUint128(units) to increase marketState[id].withdrawable before the callback fires, then fires IRepayCallback.onRepay during which the callback reenters withdraw() which calls _updatePosition and then reduces _marketState.withdrawable by the withdrawn units, so that the outer repay's already-incremented withdrawable is further decremented by the reentrant withdraw, violating the invariant that withdrawable must equal the sum of all repaid-but-not-yet-withdrawn loan tokens, causing scoped impact: withdrawable underflows or lenders can withdraw more than the actual token balance? Proof idea: assert marketState[id].withdrawable <= loanToken.balanceOf(address(this)) after any repay+withdraw sequence.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: msb / isHealthy / take] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the seller has a collateral bitmap with the highest bit set (bit 127) trigger take() which after all callbacks calls isHealthy() which uses UtilsLib.msb(bitmap) relying on the clz opcode to find bit 127, then during ISellCallback.onSell the callback calls withdrawCollateral for a lower-index collateral, clearing that bit via UtilsLib.clearBit, so that the isHealthy check after the sell callback uses a modified bitmap that excludes the withdrawn collateral, violating the invariant that the health check at the end of take must reflect the seller's actual collateral state at that moment, causing scoped impact: seller passes health check with less collateral than expected, leaving an undercollateralized debt position? Proof idea: assert isHealthy result after take matches a fresh isHealthy call with the final collateral state.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: toUint128 / take (totalUnits overflow)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that _marketState.totalUnits is near type(uint128).max trigger take() which computes _marketState.totalUnits = UtilsLib.toUint128(_marketState.totalUnits + buyerCreditIncrease - sellerCreditDecrease), then fires IBuyCallback.onBuy during which the callback reenters take() adding more buyerCreditIncrease to totalUnits, causing the outer take's UtilsLib.toUint128 to revert with CastOverflow() when it tries to cast the sum, violating the invariant that a valid take must not be DoS'd by a reentrant state inflation, causing scoped impact: attacker can cheaply DoS any take by pre-inflating totalUnits via a reentrant callback? Proof idea: fuzz totalUnits near type(uint128).max and assert take does not revert due to CastOverflow when inputs are individually valid.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / flashLoan] Can an unprivileged flash loan borrower under the precondition that they receive a flash loan of the loan token trigger flashLoan() which sends tokens and fires IFlashLoanCallback.onFlashLoan during which the callback reenters take() as a taker of a sell offer, causing the seller's liquidation lock to be set via tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true) and the take to complete (including health check), then the flash loan repayment pulls tokens back, violating the invariant that flash loan callbacks must not leave persistent transient storage state that affects subsequent calls in the same transaction, causing scoped impact: if the flash loan callback's take fails mid-execution, the liquidation lock may be left set, blocking liquidation of the seller for the rest of the transaction? Proof idea: assert liquidationLocked(id, seller) is false after any flashLoan call that includes a take in its callback.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: zeroFloorSub / take (sellerDebtIncrease post-maturity check)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that block.timestamp == market.maturity (boundary) trigger take() which computes sellerDebtIncrease = units - sellerCreditDecrease using UtilsLib.zeroFloorSub indirectly, then checks require(block.timestamp <= offer.market.maturity || sellerDebtIncrease == 0), and during IBuyCallback.onBuy the callback causes block.timestamp to advance past maturity (impossible in a single tx but testable via warp in fuzz), violating the invariant that debt must not increase after maturity, causing scoped impact: a take that was valid at timestamp == maturity creates new debt that should be forbidden? Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp at maturity boundary and assert sellerDebtIncrease == 0 for any take where block.timestamp >= market.maturity.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: mulDivDown / updatePositionView / _updatePosition] Can an unprivileged user under the precondition that a lender has credit and the market has a non-zero lossFactor trigger take() which calls _updatePosition for the buyer, computing postSlashCredit = credit.mulDivDown(type(uint128).max - marketState[id].lossFactor, type(uint128).max - _lastLossFactor), then during IBuyCallback.onBuy the callback triggers another liquidation that increases lossFactor, so that the outer take's _updatePosition used a stale lossFactor, violating the invariant that credit slashing must be applied exactly once per loss event and must use the current lossFactor, causing scoped impact: lender's credit is under-slashed because _updatePosition used a pre-liquidation lossFactor? Proof idea: assert position[id][user].credit after _updatePosition equals the correct post-slash value using the final lossFactor.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: atMostOneNonZero / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that they pass seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0 (both zero, which passes atMostOneNonZero) trigger liquidate() which skips the repaidUnits/seizedAssets computation block (if (repaidUnits > 0 || seizedAssets > 0) is false), only realizing bad debt, then fires ILiquidateCallback.onLiquidate with seizedAssets=0 and repaidUnits=0, during which the callback reenters liquidate() with the same borrower and non-zero seizedAssets, violating the invariant that bad debt must be realized exactly once and that a second liquidation on the same borrower must see the updated debt after bad debt reduction, causing scoped impact: second reentrant liquidation computes maxDebt and badDebt from already-reduced debt, potentially over-seizing collateral? Proof idea: assert that two sequential liquidations on the same borrower produce the same total outcome as a single liquidation.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: tExchange / take (seller lock and repay reentry)] Can an unprivileged seller under the precondition that they have debt and a sell callback registered trigger take() which sets tExchange(LIQUIDATION_LOCK_SLOT, id, seller, true), completes the buy callback and token transfers, then fires ISellCallback.onSell during which the sell callback calls repay() on behalf of the seller, reducing position[id][seller].debt via UtilsLib.toUint128, so that the outer take's already-committed sellerPos.debt += sellerDebtIncrease adds debt on top of the repay-reduced debt, violating the invariant that the seller's final debt must equal pre-take debt minus credit decrease plus debt increase, causing scoped impact: seller's debt is lower than expected after take because repay inside sell callback double-reduces debt? Proof idea: assert position[id][seller].debt after take equals pre_debt - sellerCreditDecrease + sellerDebtIncrease - repaid_units.

[File: 'src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: mulDivUp / take (buyerAssets overcollection)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that buyerPrice > WAD (price above par) and units is chosen such that units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) overflows uint256 trigger take() which computes buyerAssets = units.mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) without overflow protection (mulDivUp uses unchecked arithmetic in (x * y + (d - 1)) / d), causing buyerAssets to wrap to a small value, then the consumed check newConsumed <= offer.maxAssets passes with the wrapped value, violating the invariant that consumed amounts must accurately reflect the true asset value of fills, causing scoped impact: offer is filled for a large unit amount while consumed records a tiny asset amount, allowing the offer to be filled far beyond its maxAssets cap? Proof idea: fuzz units and buyerPrice near overflow boundary and assert consumed[maker][group] >= true_buyerAssets after take.
